source_jobs.title: Sales Development Rep
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CONTRA

Contra https://contra.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com is building the modern professional network for independent work. We help freelancers, studios, and independent teams find work, manage clients, and run payments, all in one place.

As more creative studios shift toward flexible, distributed teams, we’re building the infrastructure that helps them operate and scale.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Sales Development Rep with an opportunity to grow into an accountt manager to help grow Contra’s network of top creative studios. This is a relationship-driven growth role focused on sourcing and onboarding high-value studios onto Contra.

You’ll identify standout studios, build relationships with founders and operators, help them establish a strong presence on the platform, and guide them toward running their client payments and contractor payouts through Contra.

You will act as both a strategic partner and a trusted point of contact for studios as they grow on the platform. This role is ideal for someone who deeply understands the creative industry, has strong taste, is highly proactive, and enjoys building relationships from scratch.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Identify high-potential creative studios across design, branding, motion, product, development, content, and creative production

 - Source studios through platforms like X, Dribbble, Behance, LinkedIn, and referrals

 - Run outbound outreach and relationship-building with studio founders and operators

 - Own the onboarding process from first conversation through activation on Contra

 - Help studios build and optimize their Contra presence, including profiles, portfolios, services, and positioning

 - Educate studios on how to use Contra to manage payments, contractor payouts, hiring workflows, and client operations

 - Build long-term relationships with top studios and act as an ongoing strategic point of contact

 - Partner closely with product and growth teams to share feedback and identify opportunities to improve the studio experience

 - Track pipeline activity, studio activation, payment volume, and expansion opportunities




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong understanding of the creative studio ecosystem

 - Experience in partnerships, business development, talent management, recruiting, creator partnerships, or community growth

 - Exceptional written communication and relationship-building skills

 - Strong taste and ability to identify high-quality creative work

 - Comfortable with outbound outreach and relationship-driven sales

 - Highly organized and self-directed

 - Excited about the future of independent work and creative entrepreneurship

 - Bonus if you’ve worked directly with creative studios, agencies, freelancers, or creator ecosystems




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Top creative studios actively using Contra

 - Studios building high-quality profiles and portfolios on the platform

 - Increased payment volume flowing through Contra

 - Strong long-term relationships with studio founders and operators

 - Contra becoming a trusted operational platform for creative studios globally




WHY JOIN CONTRA

 - Work directly with some of the best creative talent and studios in the world

 - Help shape how modern creative teams operate

 - Join a fast-moving team building products for the future of work

 - High ownership and ability to shape the role as the ecosystem grows

 - Opportunity to grow alongside a fast-scaling team and a partnerships function




LOCATION

 - New York, United States (in office)




COMPENSATION

 - Salary
   
   - $100,000 USD base salary + uncapped commission ($120,000 USD OTE)

 - Medical, Dental, Vision Benefits

 - 401k Matching

 - We will provide you with a company laptop on your start date



Interview Process

 1. Intro Call with Recruiting Team (20 minutes)

 2. Interview with General Manager, Payments (30 minutes)

 3. CEO & Co-Founder (30 minutes)

 4. Paid Presentation + Case Study (60 minutes)

source_jobs.title: Strategic Project Lead
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Contra Labs is a human-centered research lab studying how creatives actually use AI, how tools can serve creative work better, and how to protect the originality that gets flattened as everything starts to look the same.

We connect a global network of top creative talent with frontier AI labs and product companies. By grounding our research in real creative workflows, we help define what "good" looks like across design, video, imagery, and beyond. Our work powers benchmarking, evaluation, and post-training for leading models and applications. Built on Contra, the professional network for independent creatives, we turn human judgment into the training and evaluation layer that helps these tools serve the people who use them.



Why this role exists

We are scaling across client engagements: post-training dataset creation, benchmarking programs, and ongoing evaluation partnerships. Each project spans a different mix of AI labs, creative-tool companies, and research teams, with different requirements, timelines, and quality bars. We need someone to own this work from end to end.

Think of yourself as the operator for a project or vertical. You sit between three groups that do not naturally talk to each other: the client who needs the data, the creative experts producing it, and the internal engineering team building the tooling. Your job is to make all three feel like they are working on the same thing, and to ship results that are both rigorous and genuinely useful.



What you'll own

 - The methodology. Annotation protocols, rubrics, and evaluation designs for creative and multimodal outputs.

 - Delivery. Scope, evaluator staffing, quality routing, timelines, and the client relationship, from kickoff to final readout.

 - Quality. Data quality, inter-rater reliability, and real-time course correction when something feels off. You hold the bar, and you hold the levers to fix it.

 - Analysis and storytelling. Qualitative and quantitative analysis turned into clear findings, recommendations, reports, and workshops that clients act on.

 - The systems behind it. You turn repeated work into clean, measurable process, and you partner with engineering on the tooling that makes that process faster and more reliable.

 - The narrative. Case studies and benchmark publications that sharpen how Contra Labs is understood in the market.



About you

 - You design studies for a living. You can scope a question, run the study, analyze the data, and translate it into a direction people can act on.

 - You understand good creative, and you can explain it. You recognize creative quality and can say why, and you can tell the difference between personal preference and something closer to ground truth. You build frameworks that capture that signal faithfully.

 - You care more about people than about technology for its own sake. You understand how humans judge creative work and design systems around that.

 - You turn messy work into machines. You make process clear, predictable, and measurable, and you run a tight ship across several projects at once.

 - High ownership, low ego, bias to action. You ship a v1, learn, and do what it takes to deliver.



Experience

 - 3+ years designing studies, running evaluations, analyzing data, and presenting findings.

 - Experience with data labeling, annotation, human-in-the-loop systems, or AI and ML evaluation workflows.

 - A track record managing several concurrent projects, including annotation, evaluation, or QA work.

 - A real point of view on creative quality and what "good" looks like.



Bonus

 - A strong research background, qualitative and quantitative. A Master's or PhD in HCI, cognitive science, psychology, design research, or a related field is a plus, and so is equivalent depth from industry.

 - Statistical methods for evaluation work: inter-rater reliability, sampling, regression, significance testing.

 - RLHF, LLM evaluation, or benchmark design.

 - Ethnography, UX rese
